---
title: "Online Supplement for CRPTO"
author: "Anonymous"
date: today
lang: en
bibliography: ../book/references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    number-sections: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
execute:
  freeze: auto
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Scope

This supplement is the IJDS online-supplement draft. It collects the material
that is too long for the 25-page body but still necessary for reviewer trust:
proof details, robustness tables A3--A18, reproducibility, MRM, fairness and
artifact lineage.
:::

# Appendix A: Theoretical Details

This appendix contains the formal notation and proof details that are too long
for the IJDS body. The body states the operational guarantee in words; here the
portfolio quantities are defined explicitly so that the bound check can be
reproduced from the frozen artifacts.

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `Y_i` | Realized default indicator or bounded loss proxy for loan `i`. |
| `p_hat_i` | Calibrated probability of default used by the decision layer. |
| `u_i(alpha)` | Upper conformal endpoint at miscoverage level `alpha`. |
| `x_i` | Funded decision or allocation weight. |
| `w_i` | Normalized funded-set weight used in the portfolio bound. |
| `gamma` | Policy parameter in the robust optimization rule. |
| `Gamma_CP` | Portfolio-level conformal risk metric reported after allocation. |
| `V(alpha)` | Weighted funded-set miscoverage quantity. |

For a fixed funded allocation, conformal coverage controls the expected
interval miss indicator under the stated exchangeability design. Normalized
funded weights turn loan-level misses into `V(alpha)`. A Markov-style argument
then converts the expected weighted miss control into the portfolio-level
quantity checked in the exact funded-set evaluation. Conditional
Hoeffding/Bernstein tightenings are reported as sensitivity calculations rather
than as the main theorem, because the Lending Club evaluation is temporal and
dependence-aware theory is kept as future work.

# Appendix B: P1 Evidence

The P1 evidence package records the checks used to promote CRPTO from a
promising conformal/optimization pipeline to a paper-ready result. These tables
are regenerated from frozen artifacts and are not hand-edited.

| Appendix table | Role |
|---|---|
| A3 nested holdout | Confirms the promoted result under an additional validation split. |
| A4 segment-period sensitivity | Checks whether conclusions depend on a narrow time segment. |
| A5 decision-aware selector | Compares conformal candidates through decision-facing metrics. |
| A6 synthetic shift | Stress-tests uncertainty behavior under controlled perturbations. |
| A7 funded-set loan export | Gives the reviewer a row-level funded-set audit trail. |
| A8 funded-set composition | Summarizes grade, amount, and risk composition of the chosen portfolio. |
| A9 strict temporal holdout | Separates final evidence from earlier model-selection decisions. |
| A10 finalist exact bound evaluation | Verifies exact alpha-safe status for conformal finalists. |
| A11 enhanced synthetic shift | Extends shift checks around the final promoted policy. |

# Appendix C: Journal Robustness Package

The journal robustness package addresses likely reviewer objections that do not
fit in the body without crowding the main narrative.

| Appendix table | Role |
|---|---|
| A12 tail-risk OCE/CVaR diagnostics | Shows whether tail-risk objectives tell the same qualitative story. |
| A13 satisficing margins | Measures distance from policy feasibility boundaries. |
| A14 dependency cluster diagnostics | Checks whether correlated segments drive the bound. |
| A15 leave-one-period stress | Removes one period at a time to assess temporal fragility. |
| A16 bootstrap funded-set metrics | Quantifies sampling variation in funded-set summaries. |
| A17 budget/LGD/cap sensitivity | Varies operational assumptions around budget and exposure. |
| A18 robust region by policy family | Confirms that the robust region is not tied to one narrow policy family. |

# Appendix D: Fair Lending, MRM And Governance

The governance appendix summarizes proxy and intersectional fairness audits,
model-risk-management cards, challenger criteria, and retraining triggers. The
fairness analysis is framed as a model-risk diagnostic rather than as a legal
certification. The public Lending Club data do not contain direct protected
attributes, so the paper cannot claim statutory fair-lending compliance.

The MRM material documents intended use, out-of-scope use, model assumptions,
calibration and conformal diagnostics, challenger policies, artifact lineage,
and escalation triggers. In the CRPTO setting, a retraining trigger is not an
automatic production process; it is a research governance event that would
require a new run tag and a fresh drift report against the frozen champion.

# Appendix E: Reproducibility

The reviewer-facing reproduction path separates artifact-independent checks
from artifact-aware validation. The minimal local checks are:

```powershell
just setup-base
just smoke
just tables
just figures
just journal-package
just book
```

The full artifact-aware validation additionally requires credentials for the
remote artifact store and should be run only when the review process allows the
companion bundle to be disclosed. The key invariant is the manifest hash policy:
protected model, conformal, and portfolio artifacts must match the recorded
hashes unless a new run tag and drift report are explicitly created.

The following stages must not be rerun as routine reproduction steps:
`crpto.pd.champion`, `crpto.conformal.intervals`,
`crpto.conformal.validation`, `crpto.portfolio.optimization`, and especially
`crpto.portfolio.bound_exact_eval`. Paper-facing commands such as table,
figure, evidence, journal-package, and book renders are safe because they
consume frozen inputs.
